population: 53 England Athletics Youth Talent Programme athletes; 29 endurance athletes
intervention: Co-created health and wellbeing monitoring requirements through athlete survey plus athlete and staff qualitative work
comparator: Current diary and monitoring practices without automated plan comparison
component_dose: One cross-sectional survey plus focus groups and interview
outcome_measure: Diary use; health monitoring use; preferred input time and cadence; clarity; feedback usefulness
instrument_protocol: Survey with athlete and staff focus groups or interview; participant-visible completion stages retained
missingness: Ninety survey records were incomplete across consent start and completion stages
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 62% did not keep a training diary; 45% did not monitor health or wellbeing; half of diary users reported 0-5 minutes
uncertainty: Descriptive single-programme estimates; confidence intervals not reported in reviewed extraction
individual_response: Preferred cadence and burden varied between athletes
limitations: Purposive England talent programme; age 16-18 only; preferences not longitudinal adherence; no automated prescription or safety outcome

population: 34 injured high-school athletes
intervention: Consumer-grade wearable collection with tutorials sync checks text troubleshooting GPS disabling and deidentified transfer
comparator: No automated plan or explanation comparator
component_dose: Approximately two months of daily wearable collection in two cohorts
outcome_measure: Daily wearable adherence; technical missingness; exclusion; withdrawal and support events
instrument_protocol: Device wear and sync records with cohort-specific completeness accounting
missingness: Exclusions and missing wear were reported separately; exact withdrawal count not retained in source note
adverse_events: Participants were injured at enrolment; intervention-emergent adverse events NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Median daily adherence was 93% and 95% in the two cohorts
uncertainty: Small cohorts; adherence ranges and time variation reported but exact intervals not retained in this lane
individual_response: Adherence varied by participant and over time
limitations: Small injured compensated or vendor-mediated sample; only three track athletes; no candidate explanation test; no efficacy or safety inference

population: 60 adolescents from three Australian schools
intervention: Wearable activity tracker use followed by focus groups
comparator: Pre-use expectations and reported experience; no automated prescription comparator
component_dose: Six weeks of tracker use
outcome_measure: Ease and usefulness; comfort; design; sport-specific feedback; removal and sleep friction
instrument_protocol: Qualitative focus groups after six-week use
missingness: NOT_REPORTED
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Devices were generally described as easy and useful; sport feedback comfort water-sport removal and sleep use created friction
uncertainty: Qualitative findings without quantified completion or uncertainty intervals
individual_response: Individual preferences and sport contexts differed
limitations: Non-athlete school sample; qualitative acceptability; no objective completion endpoint; no automated plan comparison or safety outcome

population: 1843 children and adolescents across 33 studies
intervention: Wearable activity tracker interventions
comparator: Heterogeneous study comparators
component_dose: Thirty-three heterogeneous studies; dose varied
outcome_measure: Attitude; burden; intervention coherence and understanding; usefulness; technical barriers; continued adherence
instrument_protocol: Systematic review of studies searched through 2019 with heterogeneous measurement and risk of bias
missingness: Variable reporting across studies; exact pooled missingness not reported
adverse_events: Adverse events were not synthesized
effect_estimate: Technical charging syncing and design barriers occurred; some studies reported adherence declining after novelty
uncertainty: Heterogeneous designs and variable risk of bias precluded a universal feasibility threshold
individual_response: Responses and adherence varied across studies and over time
limitations: Mostly non-athlete interventions; no coach-plan comparison; acceptability cannot establish efficacy safety or injury prevention

population: 144 intervention adolescents from nine schools
intervention: Twelve-week multicomponent wearable physical-activity intervention
comparator: Cluster-trial context; no automated training-plan comparator
component_dose: Wearable content notifications and weekly challenges for 12 weeks
outcome_measure: Content understanding; tracker ease; daily wear; weekly challenge completion; notification burden
instrument_protocol: Mixed-method implementation evaluation alongside cluster randomized trial
missingness: Component-level noncompletion reported; exact attrition and technical missingness not retained
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 83.3% found content easy to understand; 93.3% found tracker easy; 18.6% reported daily wear; 36.1% completed weekly challenges
uncertainty: Self-reported adherence and multicomponent attribution; confidence intervals not retained
individual_response: Engagement declined and notifications were unwelcome for some participants
limitations: Inactive non-athlete sample; stated ease did not establish comprehension or completion; no prescription comparison; no efficacy or safety transfer

population: 20 adult competitive middle- and long-distance runners
intervention: Six-week health and wellbeing monitoring prototype
comparator: Usual practice before prototype; no automated plan comparator
component_dose: Weekly monitoring for six weeks
outcome_measure: Weekly completion; item clarity; routine fit; visible usefulness; data access and control
instrument_protocol: Prototype use followed by qualitative interviews
missingness: One of 20 did not complete weekly monitoring; reasons not fully retained
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: 19 of 20 completed weekly monitoring
uncertainty: Small purposive qualitative study; no confidence interval
individual_response: Participants differed in desired definitions routine fit outputs and data-control expectations
limitations: Adult indirectness; small sample; no youth coach comparison automated prescription efficacy or safety outcome

population: 74 elite judo swimming and volleyball athletes
intervention: Online sports-health surveillance system
comparator: Existing face-to-face communication
component_dose: Repeated online reports over 15-42 weeks
outcome_measure: Response rate; feedback expectations; relationship to face-to-face communication
instrument_protocol: Mixed-method pilot with repeated online surveillance and end-user perceptions
missingness: Nonresponse was substantial; exact withdrawal and technical failure breakdown not retained
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Response rates ranged from 50% to 61%; users expected feedback and viewed the system as complementary to face-to-face contact
uncertainty: Sport and follow-up heterogeneity; descriptive response rates only
individual_response: User perceptions and response persistence varied
limitations: Adult or young-adult non-runner sample; early subjective perceptions; cannot establish prevention replacement of coach safety or efficacy

population: No athlete population; methodological examples across fields
intervention: Feasibility-study domain framework
comparator: Undifferentiated use of pilot or efficacy terminology
component_dose: Acceptability demand implementation practicality adaptation integration expansion and limited-efficacy domains
outcome_measure: Conceptual feasibility domains and example questions
instrument_protocol: Methodological framework; not a validated checklist
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: First pilot may assess acceptability implementation practicality and workflow integration; no empirical effect estimate
uncertainty: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Conceptual framework only; no universal progression threshold; limited-efficacy domain is excluded from this pilot

population: Methods research; no athlete population
intervention: International consensus and Delphi definition of feasibility and pilot studies
comparator: Inconsistent prior terminology
component_dose: Feasibility asks whether and how a future study can be done; pilot rehearses all or part of intended process at smaller scale
outcome_measure: Definition and boundary between feasibility and pilot work
instrument_protocol: International consensus or Delphi methods
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: RQ-F may test observability and process progression but not product efficacy safety or identity
uncertainty: Consensus definition; no product-specific effect estimate
limitations: RCT-preparation orientation; not automated training or product-adoption validation

population: Healthcare stakeholders across roles
intervention: Role- and risk-sensitive explanations for AI-supported decisions
comparator: Undifferentiated explanation exposure
component_dose: Inputs reasons missingness uncertainty and human authority shown according to user role
outcome_measure: User-role needs; evaluation and resolution of disagreement; teach-back concept
instrument_protocol: Multidisciplinary conceptual analysis
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_REPORTED
effect_estimate: Explanation exposure does not demonstrate comprehension; comprehension and disagreement require direct testing
uncertainty: Conceptual analysis; no validated comprehension score or effect estimate
individual_response: Explanation needs depend on role and risk
limitations: Healthcare rather than youth sport; cannot prove that explanation improves decisions efficacy or safety

population: Healthcare decision-support methods; no youth-sport population
intervention: DECIDE-AI early-stage reporting guideline
comparator: Incomplete reporting of actual workflow and human-system interaction
component_dose: Seventeen AI-specific and ten generic reporting items
outcome_measure: System version; roles; modifications; errors; deviations; workflow interaction
instrument_protocol: Delphi and consensus reporting-guideline development
missingness: Missing reporting is an audit concern; no study-level missingness estimate
adverse_events: Errors and deviations are reporting items rather than safety outcomes
effect_estimate: Supports version freezing and logging of roles failures and deviations while coach authority remains explicit
uncertainty: Reporting guideline; no effect estimate or conduct threshold
limitations: Clinical setting differs from sport; reporting quality is not proof of benefit safety or efficacy

population: 75 prospective noninterventional healthcare evaluations
intervention: Prospective silent or shadow evaluation generating outputs without influencing care
comparator: Usual clinical care remains authoritative
component_dose: Seventy-five included evaluations with heterogeneous implementations
outcome_measure: Output logging; influence on care; nomenclature; user and consumer involvement
instrument_protocol: Scoping review of English-language reports from 2015-2025
missingness: Human factors and patient or consumer engagement were sparsely reported
adverse_events: Adverse events were not synthesized as a product safety outcome
effect_estimate: Silent trials commonly generated parallel outputs without influencing care; visibility varied
uncertainty: Heterogeneous terminology and reporting; one screened-total inconsistency retained as limitation
individual_response: Human and consumer visibility varied between evaluations
limitations: Healthcare indirectness; not an efficacy or safety test; TRAINORACLE must be participant-visible and cannot claim a conventional concealed silent trial

population: Children and adolescents in health-related research
intervention: CIOMS Guideline 17 on age-appropriate assent permission and withdrawal
comparator: No universal local-law comparator
component_dose: Ongoing assent with required parental or legal permission according to context
outcome_measure: Age- and maturity-tailored information; refusal; continuing assent; re-consent at majority
instrument_protocol: Official international ethics guidance
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Protection from research harm is an ethics obligation; not an observed adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Refusal or withdrawal should be respected and penalty-free; local ethics and legal determination is required before recruitment
uncertainty: Guidance rather than empirical outcome evidence; jurisdiction determines implementation
individual_response: Understanding and willingness must be assessed individually rather than inferred from age
limitations: Global guidance is not local approval or recruitment authority; supplies no efficacy safety or product identity claim

population: Children in research
intervention: UK Health Research Authority public guidance for research involving children
comparator: No universal cross-jurisdiction comparator
component_dose: Age-appropriate information and respect for refusal or withdrawal
outcome_measure: Understanding; presentation; assent; refusal and withdrawal
instrument_protocol: Official UK public guidance
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Protection obligations described; no observed adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Comprehension should be maximized and tested; withdrawal must be respected
uncertainty: Guidance rather than empirical estimate
individual_response: Understanding and assent are individual and ongoing
limitations: UK-specific; not project approval or universal law; no efficacy safety recruitment or product-identity authority

population: Children and adolescent athletes
intervention: Clinical review of overuse injury overtraining and burnout in young athletes
comparator: No prediction-model comparator
component_dose: Repetitive stress and recovery considered at individual clinical level
outcome_measure: Progression of pain after activity during activity with limitation and at rest; sleep fatigue and mood concerns
instrument_protocol: AAP clinical report; clinical assessment remains with qualified professionals
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Overuse injuries and burnout are clinical outcomes; no product adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Any explicit current pain functional limitation or suspected overuse concern supports candidate suppression and clinical review
uncertainty: Individual variation emphasized; no validated software threshold
limitations: Clinical guidance rather than prediction model; not runner-only; cannot diagnose overtraining classify benign pain prescribe rest or establish safety

population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
intervention: Updated Female Athlete Triad state-of-science model
comparator: Earlier Triad model and broader RED-S construct context
component_dose: Energy deficiency reproductive function bone health and bone-stress injury integrated clinically
outcome_measure: Energy-deficiency reproductive-function bone-health and bone-stress-injury domains; uncertainty of recovery
instrument_protocol: Consensus and state-of-science review requiring clinical measurement and interpretation
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Bone-stress injury is a clinical outcome; adverse events not synthesized for product use
effect_estimate: Sex-specific evidence supports an optional structured concern route but no automated label score or restriction
uncertainty: Complete bone-health recovery remains uncertain; evidence is construct- and measure-dependent
limitations: Female-specific clinical construct; no product validation; cannot infer concern from sex weight pace training data or private-note text

population: Adolescent and adult female athletes
intervention: Clinical screening diagnosis treatment and return-to-play consensus guidance
comparator: Prior threshold-oriented approaches
component_dose: Multidisciplinary clinical assessment; no universal single energy-availability threshold
outcome_measure: Clinical screening diagnosis treatment and participation decisions
instrument_protocol: Consensus guideline dependent on medical data and multidisciplinary interpretation
missingness: Unanswered required structured concern is unresolved rather than negative
adverse_events: Clinical outcomes discussed; no product adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Explicit concern or unresolved required answer may suppress a candidate and route to a human; no threshold point system or return-to-play automation
uncertainty: Clinical risk categories are not consumer-software validated and no universal threshold applies
limitations: Female-specific and medical-data dependent; no automated menstrual body-mass energy or return-to-play rule; no safety or readiness inference

population: Adolescent and young adult male athletes; especially endurance and weight-class sports
intervention: Male Athlete Triad clinical consensus
comparator: Assumption that male sex is protective
component_dose: Multidisciplinary screening diagnosis treatment and return-to-play framework
outcome_measure: Energy deficiency reproductive-axis suppression impaired bone health and participation decisions
instrument_protocol: Abstract-limited coalition consensus
missingness: Full-text details not available in this lane
adverse_events: Clinical outcomes discussed; adverse-event frequencies not available
effect_estimate: Male athletes can experience the Triad domains; the same non-diagnostic concern and referral path applies across sexes
uncertainty: Smaller male evidence base and consensus extrapolation; no validated youth-running classifier
limitations: Abstract-only; cannot automate screening diagnosis score or return-to-play; sex cannot clear or restrict a candidate

population: Healthy children and adolescents
intervention: Age-banded regular sleep-duration consensus
comparator: No athlete-session clearance comparator
component_dose: Ages 6-12: 9-12 hours regularly; ages 13-18: 8-10 hours regularly
outcome_measure: Regular sleep duration associated with general health
instrument_protocol: Modified RAND consensus process
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Consensus ranges support general health only; persistent concern may route to a human
uncertainty: Consensus recommendations are not athlete injury recovery or session-clearance thresholds
limitations: General paediatric rather than athlete evidence; one night cannot become injury prediction readiness medical stop or clearance

population: Athletes with acute respiratory infection
intervention: IOC clinical assessment and return-to-sport framework
comparator: Consumer symptom checker or elapsed-time clearance
component_dose: Infection severity athlete health risk activity risk and symptom monitoring considered clinically
outcome_measure: Current illness; fever or chills; chest pain; breathing difficulty; fainting; severe or unusual fatigue
instrument_protocol: Abstract and accessible publisher sections of IOC consensus; clinician-led assessment
missingness: Full-text detail limited in this extraction lane
adverse_events: Clinical illness outcomes discussed; no product adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Any explicit illness or listed serious concern suppresses candidate generation and routes to qualified care
uncertainty: Broad population and respiratory focus; no validated consumer threshold
limitations: Youth runner directness low; no diagnosis severity grading neck-check myocarditis inference treatment or return-to-sport algorithm

population: Adolescent athletes ages 12-18
intervention: Team-physician consensus for adolescent athlete health
comparator: Product-led maturity or medical clearance
component_dose: Growth development medical musculoskeletal psychological overtraining and return-to-sport assessment
outcome_measure: Age and development scope; medical and musculoskeletal concern; psychological and overtraining context
instrument_protocol: Abstract-limited multidisciplinary consensus
missingness: Full text not retrieved in this lane
adverse_events: Clinical outcomes discussed; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Unknown age or development context and any health concern require qualified human review
uncertainty: Consensus not prediction model; children under 12 explicitly outside scope
limitations: Abstract-only; cannot estimate maturity diagnose or substitute for team physician; under-12 transfer unresolved

population: Athletes across ages and levels; children and youth explicitly considered
intervention: Athlete-centred whole-system trauma- and violence-informed safeguarding framework
comparator: Model classification or default coach disclosure
component_dose: Forty-three consensus statements plus accessible response mechanisms
outcome_measure: Accessible response route; trained safeguarding lead; athlete-centred response; local duties
instrument_protocol: Scoping review plus Delphi consensus
missingness: Narrative disclosure is not required and remains outside planner; route metadata only after privacy approval
adverse_events: Interpersonal violence and maltreatment are safeguarding harms; no automated adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: A visible REQUEST_HUMAN_SUPPORT or structured concern must route outside the planner to a trained human
uncertainty: Several statements lacked consensus; legal and reporting duties vary by jurisdiction
individual_response: Participant choice concern context and route needs vary
limitations: Complex partly subjective domain; no abuse classification credibility score coach disclosure by default or plan input; private note is zero-signal

population: Children participating in sport from grassroots through elite
intervention: Eight international organizational safeguards for children in sport
comparator: No safeguarding owner or untested software-only route
component_dose: Policy concern-response procedures support risk management behaviour recruitment training partnerships and monitoring
outcome_measure: Named owner; tested response procedure; advice and support; monitoring
instrument_protocol: Official organizational standard
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Safeguarding harms are in scope but no empirical event-rate estimate is supplied
effect_estimate: A real named safeguarding owner and tested procedure are prerequisites; software cannot be the safeguarding officer
uncertainty: Organizational standard rather than outcome study; jurisdiction-specific implementation
limitations: Empirical authority lower than systematic review; no automated classification notification or cross-jurisdiction reporting rule

population: Elite youth athletes across 30 eligible pathway studies
intervention: Observed growth and maturation exposures in elite youth pathways
comparator: Different maturity and growth definitions across studies
component_dose: Thirty eligible studies with heterogeneous maturity and injury methods
outcome_measure: Injury incidence and burden by maturity status; growth-spurt timing; stature and lower-limb growth rate
instrument_protocol: Scoping review with heterogeneous study definitions
missingness: Female athletes and sport diversity underrepresented; outcome reporting heterogeneous
adverse_events: Injury outcomes synthesized descriptively; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Incidence or burden generally increased with maturity status; growth-related injuries peaked around growth spurt; faster growth associated with greater incidence or burden; maturity timing inconsistent
uncertainty: No pooled product threshold; heterogeneous samples measures and definitions
individual_response: Associations differed by maturity measure sport and study
limitations: Mixed-sport elite pathways; association cannot estimate individual maturity injury probability clearance or safety

population: Male elite adolescent athletics academy athletes; 117 athlete-seasons
intervention: Prospective observation of stature leg-length growth and skeletal maturity
comparator: Between-athlete growth-rate differences
component_dose: One academy cohort across 117 athlete-seasons
outcome_measure: Bone-injury and growth-plate-injury incidence-rate ratios
instrument_protocol: Prospective cohort; abstract-limited growth and injury measures
missingness: Full-text missingness not available
adverse_events: Bone and growth-plate injuries observed prospectively
effect_estimate: Per 1 SD higher stature growth rate: bone-injury IRR 1.5 and growth-plate IRR 2.1; leg-length growth also associated; greater skeletal maturity associated with fewer growth-plate injuries
uncertainty: 95% CI 1.1-1.9 for bone IRR and 1.5-3.1 for growth-plate IRR
individual_response: Individual response not reported; findings are cohort associations
limitations: Abstract-only; small male elite single academy; observational multiple measures and outcomes; IRRs cannot become individual risk maturity or clearance score

population: 748 competitive high-school runners at baseline; follow-up for 428 girls and 273 boys
intervention: Prospective observation of stress fractures and baseline factors
comparator: Sex-stratified baseline-factor comparisons
component_dose: Mean follow-up 2.3 seasons
outcome_measure: Prospective stress fracture occurrence; prior fracture; reported sex-specific correlates
instrument_protocol: Prospective cohort; abstract-limited baseline self-report and injury follow-up
missingness: Follow-up data unavailable for 14 girls and 33 boys relative to baseline counts; reasons not retained
adverse_events: 23 stress fractures in girls and 11 in boys
effect_estimate: Stress fractures occurred in 5.4% of followed girls and 4.0% of followed boys; prior fracture was strongest factor in both sexes; female-specific associations included BMI below 19 later menarche and gymnastics or dance
uncertainty: Low event counts and multivariable observational uncertainty; confidence intervals not retained
individual_response: Sex-specific factors differed; no individual predictive validity established
limitations: Abstract-only; older cohort self-reported factors low events; no causal thresholds; BMI sex menarche or sport history must not become automated score restriction or diagnosis

population: High-school and collegiate cross-country runners in prospective cohorts
intervention: Systematic review of prospective running-injury risk-factor cohorts
comparator: Heterogeneous risk-factor definitions and populations
component_dose: Study-specific training sleep specialization injury history and RED-S-related factors
outcome_measure: Sex; prior injury; RED-S-related risk; training sleep and specialization factors
instrument_protocol: Systematic review; abstract-limited full methods and estimates
missingness: Study-level missingness not available from abstract
adverse_events: Running-related injuries synthesized; no product adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Sex and prior running-related injury were strongest predictors; moderate certainty for association of greater RED-S-related risk with injury especially BSI; low certainty for training sleep and specialization
uncertainty: Certainty varied by factor; numerical pooled estimates not retained
limitations: Abstract-only; mixed high-school and collegiate population; heterogeneous definitions; association cannot diagnose recurrence predict injury or justify readiness clearance

population: Adolescent athletes
intervention: Narrative review of BSI risk diagnosis and management
comparator: Heterogeneous underlying adolescent studies
component_dose: Load energy balance bone health biomechanics and psychological context considered clinically
outcome_measure: Incidence recurrence diagnosis and multidisciplinary management context
instrument_protocol: Narrative review of heterogeneous definitions and denominators
missingness: Minimal paediatric empirical evidence and heterogeneous reporting
adverse_events: BSI incidence and recurrence reported across included populations
effect_estimate: Reported BSI incidence ranges 3.9%-19% and recurrence as high as 21% across cited adolescent populations
uncertainty: Ranges are heterogeneous and unsuitable for individual prediction
limitations: Narrative non-runner-only review; heterogeneous diagnosis and denominators; cannot infer BSI from load or provide diagnosis treatment management readiness or safety

population: Children and adolescents in repetitive youth sports; 448 reported patients across 128 reports
intervention: Systematic review of primary periphyseal stress injuries
comparator: Heterogeneous case reports case series and other reports
component_dose: One hundred one of 128 reports were case reports or series
outcome_measure: Growth disturbance surgery delayed presentation symptoms and imaging findings
instrument_protocol: Systematic review; abstract-limited and case-report dominated
missingness: Prospective incidence data sparse; case ascertainment selective
adverse_events: 57 of 448 reported patients had growth disturbance and 28 underwent surgery
effect_estimate: 12.7% growth disturbance and 6.2% surgery among reported patients; delayed presentation appeared in harmful cases; some imaging changes were asymptomatic
uncertainty: Selection and publication bias make proportions non-population risks
limitations: Abstract-only; case-report dominance mixed sports no symptom-screen validation; product cannot infer imaging diagnosis severity treatment or individual probability

population: Athletes age 19 or younger across seven studies
intervention: Observed chronic or acute sleep exposure
comparator: Better sleep exposure within observational studies
component_dose: Seven heterogeneous studies
outcome_measure: Sports injury association for chronic poor sleep and acute sleep
instrument_protocol: Random-effects systematic review and meta-analysis; abstract-limited
missingness: Study-level missingness not available
adverse_events: Sports injuries were outcomes; no product adverse-event rate
effect_estimate: Chronic poor sleep associated with injury: random-effects OR 1.58; two acute-sleep studies conflicted
uncertainty: 95% CI 1.05-2.37; only seven studies; Level IV observational synthesis
limitations: Abstract-only; self-report and inconsistent definitions; association cannot make one poor night an injury prediction readiness clearance or medical stop

population: Growing children and adolescents with apophyseal or osteochondral pain
intervention: Clinical review of common growing-bone pain conditions
comparator: No questionnaire diagnosis comparator
component_dose: History examination and sometimes imaging determine clinical diagnosis
outcome_measure: Localized tenderness; limp; activity limitation; persistent pain; suspected serious pathology
instrument_protocol: Peer-reviewed clinical review with official open full text
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: Growth-related pain conditions discussed; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Running can affect hip knee and ankle growth-related sites; persistent pain or suspected serious pathology warrants clinical evaluation
uncertainty: Much guidance is expert opinion or consensus without trials
limitations: Diagnosis-specific management is not product-transferable; no apophysitis tendon diagnosis imaging inference treatment or permission to run

population: Conceptual discussion focused on female athletes; male and diverse-athlete evidence gaps noted
intervention: Editorial critique of the original 2014 RED-S construct
comparator: Original IOC RED-S consensus interpretation
component_dose: Conceptual comparison of RED-S and Female Athlete Triad models
outcome_measure: Scope and evidentiary claims of RED-S versus Triad
instrument_protocol: Peer-reviewed editorial commentary
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Authors argued the original 2014 RED-S construct overreached then-available evidence and could obscure the Triad
uncertainty: Polemical commentary rather than comparative validation; addresses 2014 not 2023 model
limitations: Conceptual dispute reinforces clinician-facing referral and uncertainty; cannot automate labels scores thresholds or allegiance to either model

population: Youth athletes across participation and performance levels
intervention: IOC healthy inclusive sustainable and enjoyable youth-development principles
comparator: Blanket age sex body-size or performance exclusion
component_dose: Individual development safeguarding participation benefit and athlete autonomy retained
outcome_measure: Healthy sustainable development; individual pathways; non-participation and uncertainty
instrument_protocol: IOC consensus statement
missingness: NOT_APPLICABLE
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Supports human-centred boundaries and respect for non-participation; supplies no Formation effect estimate
uncertainty: Broad expert principles with no runner-specific dose or referral threshold
individual_response: Individual development is nonlinear and cannot be inferred from age
limitations: Broad multi-sport consensus; no 9.5-day efficacy safety prescription clearance or blanket exclusion authority

population: Children and adolescent runners; evidence strongest in adolescents
intervention: International youth-running consensus on individualized training nutrition health and participation decisions
comparator: Opinion-based age-distance recommendations
component_dose: Individual and clinically judged decisions; no validated age-to-distance ceiling
outcome_measure: Evidence gaps; distance recommendations; pain illness nutrition energy and participation concerns
instrument_protocol: Consensus informed by literature and expert review
missingness: Preadolescent and puberty-specific evidence is especially sparse
adverse_events: Injury and health concerns discussed; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Suitable running distance by age is opinion-based and evidence is incomplete; explicit concerns support human review
uncertainty: No validated age-distance injury-prediction or clearance rule
limitations: Consensus with limited mostly adolescent evidence; cannot establish Formation efficacy safety causal thresholds age mileage permission or clearance

population: 113 competitive adolescent distance runners from England Athletics clubs
intervention: Retrospective online questionnaire of running-related injury and training practices
comparator: Sex and age-group descriptive comparisons; no intervention
component_dose: One questionnaire completed April-December 2018 about previous 12 months
outcome_measure: RRI incidence proportion and rate; body area; overuse mechanism; training frequency volume intensity; specialization
instrument_protocol: Cross-sectional self-report questionnaire with incidence proportion and participation-hour rate calculations
missingness: Target sample was 151 and 113 participated; item-level missingness not retained
adverse_events: Running-related injuries were the observed outcome
effect_estimate: All-RRI incidence proportion 68% and rate 6.3 per 1000 participation hours; knee foot or toes and lower leg common; 58% reported high specialization
uncertainty: 95% CI 60%-77% for incidence proportion and 5.3-7.4 per 1000 hours for incidence rate
individual_response: Training frequency increased with chronological age; individual trajectories not measured
limitations: Retrospective cross-sectional self-report; no maturity measurement; descriptive association only; cannot produce safe load injury prevention causal threshold readiness or safety inference

population: Female and male athletes across sports and ages with low-energy-availability context
intervention: Correction-aware IOC RED-S clinical consensus and CAT2 framework
comparator: Single-threshold or automated consumer classification
component_dose: Clinical syndrome assessment and sport-participation decisions by trained multidisciplinary team
outcome_measure: Health and performance consequences; clinician-facing assessment and participation framework
instrument_protocol: Official consensus article controlled by linked 2024 correction PMID 38325885
missingness: Original identity requires linked correction; extraction must use corrected text
adverse_events: Clinical syndrome consequences discussed; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Explicit fueling eating menstrual or hormonal recurrent bone-stress or clinician concern may only trigger qualified human review
uncertainty: Heterogeneous evolving construct with no single universally valid diagnostic threshold
limitations: Corrected consensus not youth-runner specific; CAT2 cannot be automated; no diagnosis body-size inference readiness return-to-play or safety claim; private notes remain zero-signal

population: Elite youth athletes approaching international competition
intervention: Child-centred elite-youth competition framework
comparator: Recent competition treated as automated recovery clearance
component_dose: Individual pathways and protection from injury illness harassment and abuse
outcome_measure: Developmental context; schedule facts; injury illness harassment and abuse protections
instrument_protocol: IOC expert consensus
missingness: Missing or incompatible competition facts require coach review
adverse_events: Health and safeguarding harms discussed; no product adverse-event estimate
effect_estimate: Recent competition is only a factual schedule anchor; it cannot prove recovery readiness or next-MAIN clearance
uncertainty: Elite Olympic context and expert synthesis; no Formation-specific test
limitations: Indirect to ordinary youth runners; no automated post-competition clearance efficacy safety or 9.5-day superiority claim

population: Systematic-review reporting; no athlete population
intervention: PRISMA 2020 transparent review reporting framework
comparator: Incomplete search screening and exclusion reporting
component_dose: Reporting checklist and flow structure
outcome_measure: Search sources dates screening exclusions flow and amendments
instrument_protocol: Official PRISMA 2020 reporting guidance
missingness: Missing databases and inaccessible full texts must be disclosed
adverse_events: NOT_APPLICABLE
effect_estimate: Supports auditable search screening and exclusion reporting only; no sports-science effect estimate
uncertainty: NOT_APPLICABLE
limitations: Reporting guidance does not appraise evidence establish pilot progression validate 9.5 days or support efficacy safety or prescription authority
